summary: Jeff Atwood shares a brief but pointed observation from John Carmack about his experiments with Java game development on mobile phones. The post highlights Carmack's frustration with performance issues, specifically the absurdity of text editing being slow on powerful hardware. Atwood endorses this sentiment as exceptionally quotable, suggesting it captures a fundamental truth about software bloat and performance expectations.
key_points: John Carmack experimented with cellphone game development using Java
Even basic operations like text editing can be inexplicably slow on powerful modern hardware
Software performance expectations often don't match hardware capabilities
money_quotes: there is something deeply wrong when text editing on a 3.6 ghz processor is anything but instantaneous
key_insight: The gap between raw hardware power and actual software performance reveals deep inefficiencies in how we build software.
